semantic.role: Implementation of a lock worker script for managing file-based distributed locks in the ArchSpine system.
responsibilities: Provides a CLI tool to acquire and release locks via LockManager.
Supports three modes: acquire-release, acquire-hold, and acquire-release-rewrite.
Handles process signals (SIGINT, SIGTERM) for graceful shutdown.
Emits structured output (JSON) for parent process communication.
Manages process hold-open timers to prevent premature exit.
outOfScope: Network-based locking or distributed consensus algorithms.
Configuration file parsing or environment setup.

English.purpose: This document defines the lock worker script used by larger ArchSpine processes to acquire and release file-system locks. It serves as a standalone executable that communicates via stdout JSON messages.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers integrating or debugging the locking subsystem in ArchSpine. It provides a low-level utility that can be called in scripts or test suites.
key_takeaways: The lock worker acquires/releases locks using LockManager with multiple operational modes.
It supports three modes: immediate acquire-release, acquire-hold with a configurable hold duration, and acquire-release-rewrite for rewrite operations.
Signal handlers ensure clean exit on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
Errors are captured and emitted as JSON with error codes.
The script is intended to be invoked as a child process, not used as a module.
